note: Every program from faithandresults.com, one object each, enriched from the WordPress export. Edit here; the app renders this file. 'trainings' lists ids from trainings.json that let people apply into the program.

pillars.name: Economic Development
pillars.blurb: Financial empowerment with the institutions that move money.

pillars.name: Education & Training
pillars.blurb: Workforce and business training designed and delivered nationally.

pillars.name: Housing
pillars.blurb: Homes saved, homes built, and pathways to ownership.

pillars.name: Sustainability
pillars.blurb: Energy, capacity, and communities that renew themselves.

pillars.name: Community & Health
pillars.blurb: The pro bono work: health, healing, justice, and standing with neighbors.

programs.name: Homes Saved By Faith
programs.body: An amalgam of faith-based organizations joined together to stem the tide of foreclosures across denominational, ethnic, cultural, and religious lines. Workshops, direct intervention for delinquent homeowners, credit rebuilding, and trusted alternatives to foreclosure, delivered with the CT Department of Banking, the CT Fair Housing Center, the FDIC, and the Federal Home Loan Bank.

programs.name: Get Home Now!
programs.tag: Rehab lending · vacant homes to owned homes
programs.body: A flexible package of mortgage products and development oversight that encourages rehabilitation of vacant, affordable homes, letting buyers and existing residents borrow up to 110 percent of home value to finance repairs, renovation, and acquisition, raising the whole community's housing stock.

programs.name: Homebuyer Education & Counseling
programs.body: From first conversation to keys: homebuyer education, credit preparation, and one-on-one counseling: the pathway programs FREEDOM has run city by city with Freddie Mac, HomeFree USA, the Home Ownership Training Center, and church networks from Bridgeport to Oakland.

programs.name: The Shiloh Bethel Centre
programs.body: A 66-unit mixed-use apartment development in Bridgeport: four floors over a podium, studios through two-bedrooms with balconies, six live/work units, roughly 4,000 square feet of commercial and retail space, conference space, and covered parking. Faith-anchored development at neighborhood scale.

programs.name: Financial FREEDOM
programs.body: Financial empowerment for individuals, families, and organizations: workshops and trainings presented with FDIC and HUD certified counselors in local venues, covering credit utilization, credit scores, and the tools of household wealth.

programs.name: Freddie Mac Faith Empowerment Series
programs.body: Statewide financial empowerment with Freddie Mac, anchored by the LouLease Lease Purchase Plus program, managed by FREEDOM affiliate Vision Financial Services and sponsored by the Louisiana Public Facilities Authority, offering credit-impaired and down-payment-constrained families a market-rate path to ownership.

programs.name: MLK Corridors Initiative
programs.body: A response to adverse conditions in neighborhoods whose streets carry Dr. King's name: an intentionally inclusive planning process focused on economic development, education, housing, and workforce development. Corridor communities named in Norwalk, Middletown, and New Britain, launched January 18, 2019 at Spear Park in Middletown, in collaboration with the office of the Governor and the CT Department of Banking.
partners: Black Ministers Alliance of New Britain
Office of the Governor of Connecticut
Municipalities of Norwalk, Middletown & New Britain

programs.name: Health, Wealth, Legacy
programs.body: Affordable health and wellness insurance solutions for congregations, organizations, and businesses. The FREEDOM Health platform accesses a national healthcare network so members and employees can find coverage that matches their needs and their budgets.

programs.name: CFJC Workforce Readiness Program
programs.body: Four-week pre-employment apprenticeship training matched to employers' hiring schedules: advanced manufacturing, building trades, medical, clerical, customer service, high tech, insurance, banking & finance, and bio-tech, with recruitment, screening, life-skills training, mock interviews, and DOL-compliant certified instruction.

programs.name: NeighborWorks America Training Institutes
programs.body: Pastor McCluster trains for NeighborWorks America, the country's largest HUD housing intermediary, with Training Institutes delivered in Chicago, Louisville, New York, Sacramento, Providence, Atlanta, Portland, New Orleans, Boston, Washington D.C., and Cleveland.

programs.name: Capacity Building Training
programs.body: FREEDOM is the creator, sponsor, and trainer of faith-based organizations nationwide: governance, program design, and revenue that renews itself, so the mission outlasts any single grant. Delivered through the Faith Community Development Corporation, chartered by Shiloh Baptist Church.

programs.name: Congregational Sustainability & Solar
programs.body: FREEDOM partnered with Shiloh Baptist Church to make it the first African-American house of worship in Connecticut completely powered by solar energy. Then it expanded: supporting Energize CT to lower energy costs, partnering with the Connecticut Green Bank and municipalities, and working with Posigen and other solar organizations in California, Georgia, Louisiana, Maryland, Massachusetts, and Rhode Island.

programs.name: Net Zero Communities & Tiny Homes
programs.body: The next chapter of FREEDOM's development work: Tiny Homes initiatives, urban in-fill housing development, and Net Zero Communities: self-sufficient, off-the-grid neighborhoods composed entirely of homes with zero carbon-footprint impact.

programs.name: Saving America's Soul
programs.body: Taking up the Southern Christian Leadership Conference's 1957 founding motto, "to save the soul of America," the NFLC convenes faith, community, education, business, and elected leaders to facilitate national healing and reconciliation, with supporting organizations from the SCLC and National Baptist Convention U.S.A. to the RAC of Reform Judaism and Indiaspora.

programs.name: Fighting Fear with Faith and Facts™
programs.body: The COVID-era vaccination partnership launched in Connecticut with Hartford HealthCare, taking trusted health information through pulpits instead of press releases. Phase 1 cities: Atlanta, Chicago, Colorado, Connecticut, New Orleans, New York, the San Francisco Bay Area, and Washington D.C.

programs.name: Community, Law Enforcement & Global Response
programs.body: The standing pro bono work: the annual Connecticut Statewide Community and Law Enforcement Engagement Summits, youth mentoring, community health and wellness, and global solidarity, from 2022's "United We Stand" month for the people of Ukraine to the Equity Uprise movement anchored at Shiloh, whose unified community front was recognized as a major factor when the Connecticut Siting Council denied United Illuminating's Docket 516R monopole application in October 2025.
